title: elymus repens subsp. littoreus
intro: Elymus repens subsp. littoreus, also known as sea couch grass, is a coastal subspecies of the common couch grass plant, a perennial grass species native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Elymus repens subsp. littoreus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support wound healing. Elymus repens subsp. littoreus can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and it is also used in some traditional herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to support skin health.
